findings.title: Proposal generation must integrate into existing SKILL.md step flow without breaking sprint dispatch
findings.description: implementation-plan is invoked by /dso:sprint via Skill tool. Sprint's impl-plan-dispatch.md references 'Steps 1-6'. Proposal generation must not break this.
findings.rationale: Sprint parses STATUS output protocol from implementation-plan. New steps could break step numbering references.

findings.title: CLAUDE.md agent table must be updated with new agent entry
findings.description: CLAUDE.md has Named-agent dispatch table listing every agent. Story 2 mentions agent-routing.conf but not CLAUDE.md table.
findings.rationale: CLAUDE.md table is read by all skills/orchestrators. Named agents go in CLAUDE.md table, not necessarily agent-routing.conf (which is for task-category routing).

findings.title: Sprint invocation bypasses interactive proposal presentation
findings.description: When implementation-plan is invoked from sprint, proposal selection must be non-interactive. Sprint does not handle user-facing proposal steps. Decision-maker handles selection autonomously in sprint context.
findings.rationale: Sprint invocations would hang waiting for user input if proposals are presented interactively.

findings.title: Resolution loop state file naming convention
findings.description: Use /tmp/approach-resolution-<story-id>.json for cycle count. Verify no collision with existing /tmp files. Define TTL and cleanup.
findings.rationale: implementation-plan already writes /tmp/preplanning-context files. Needs coordinated naming.

findings.title: Docs story must update sprint dispatch prompt if step numbering changes
findings.description: impl-plan-dispatch.md references Steps 1-6 explicitly. If proposal step shifts numbering, dispatch prompt must be updated.
findings.rationale: Sprint sub-agents would execute wrong steps with stale dispatch prompt.

findings.title: Update test-sub-agent-guard.sh and existing implementation-plan tests for new agent dispatch
findings.description: 6+ test files directly test implementation-plan behavior. Proposal generation and agent dispatch may break grep-based assertions and step-count assumptions.
findings.rationale: Structural changes to SKILL.md could break existing test suites that weren't updated.

rejected.title: Agent definition must follow existing agent file conventions in plugins/dso/agents/
rejected.description: All 19 existing agents are .md files in plugins/dso/agents/. discover-agents.sh depends on this convention.
rejected.rationale: Agent placed elsewhere would not be discoverable.
rejected.rejection_rationale: Single-story internal scope concern. Story 2's done definition (creating the agent) implicitly includes making it discoverable. File placement is an implementation-level detail within Story 2's own scope, not a cross-story gap. This is implementation-plan-level, not preplanning-level.

rejected.title: Agent dispatch requires SUB-AGENT-GUARD awareness
rejected.description: implementation-plan has SUB-AGENT-GUARD. New agent dispatch must work in same execution context as existing Step 6 gap analysis dispatch.
rejected.rationale: Adding another Agent tool dependency is compatible with existing guard but should be verified.
rejected.rejection_rationale: Not actionable. The finding itself concludes compatibility ('is compatible with existing guard'). The only suggested remediation is 'should be verified,' which is speculative and not a concrete change. No specific modification to any story is warranted based on this finding.
